class_schedule.topic: Capstone Project Overview and Team Formation
learning_objectives: Understand capstone project requirements and evaluation criteria
Form project teams and select project topics
Develop project proposals with defined scope and deliverables

lecture_notes.introduction: The capstone project is the culmination of your cybersecurity education. You will apply knowledge from across the curriculum to solve a real-world security problem.
key_concepts: Project categories: security tool development, red/blue team exercise, research, security architecture
Team roles: project manager, lead researcher, technical lead, documentation lead
Project proposal requirements: problem statement, scope, methodology, timeline
Industry mentorship program for real-world guidance
Milestone-based evaluation with peer review
lecture_notes.demonstration: Review of outstanding past capstone projects and discussion of what made them successful

review.value: Scope must be achievable in 15 weeks with a team of 3-4 students. Must demonstrate integration of multiple cybersecurity domains. Must produce a tangible deliverable (tool, report, architecture).
Technical depth and correctness, originality and innovation, practical applicability, documentation quality, presentation and communication, team collaboration.

learning_objectives: Conduct a systematic literature review in cybersecurity
Design experimental methodology for security research
readings: IEEE and ACM cybersecurity conference proceedings

lecture_notes.introduction: Whether building a tool or conducting research, a systematic approach grounded in existing literature ensures your work advances the field rather than duplicating past efforts.
key_concepts: Systematic literature review methodology
Research question formulation: specific, measurable, achievable
Experimental design for security research
Ethical considerations in security research
Academic writing for cybersecurity
lecture_notes.demonstration: Conducting a literature review using Google Scholar, IEEE Xplore, and ACM Digital Library on a sample cybersecurity topic

review.value: Identify existing solutions, their limitations, and the gap your project fills. Minimum 15-20 relevant papers. Organize by theme, methodology, and findings.

Security research may involve testing attacks, analyzing malware, or collecting data. All research must be conducted ethically within approved environments and comply with IRB requirements if human subjects are involved.

learning_objectives: Create detailed project plans with milestones and dependencies
Design system architectures for security tools and platforms
readings: Agile Project Management for Security Projects

lecture_notes.introduction: A well-planned project is half completed. Detailed planning with realistic milestones, risk identification, and contingency plans prevents last-minute scrambles.
key_concepts: Project planning: WBS, Gantt charts, critical path identification
Architecture design: modular, extensible, documented
Development workflow: version control, CI/CD, code review
Risk management for the project itself: scope creep, technical blockers
Communication plan: team meetings, advisor check-ins, documentation
lecture_notes.demonstration: Creating a project plan with milestones, architecture design document, and development environment setup

review.value: Week 3: Proposal approved. Week 5: Prototype/POC complete. Week 8: Core implementation done. Week 11: Testing and refinement. Week 13: Documentation complete. Week 15: Final presentation.
Define minimum viable deliverable and stretch goals separately. If the team hits blockers, the minimum deliverable is protected while stretch goals are sacrificed.

learning_objectives: Present and defend project proposals to a faculty panel
Receive and incorporate feedback on scope and methodology
Finalize project plans based on panel recommendations

lecture_notes.introduction: The proposal defense is your opportunity to demonstrate that your project is well-conceived, properly scoped, and achievable. The faculty panel provides expert feedback to strengthen your approach.
key_concepts: Proposal presentation: problem, approach, timeline, expected outcomes
Defending technical decisions with evidence
Incorporating feedback constructively
Revised proposal submission after defense
Advisor assignment based on project topic
lecture_notes.demonstration: Mock proposal defense with instructor feedback on common pitfalls

review.value: Scope too broad (trying to solve everything), scope too narrow (insufficient depth), unclear methodology (how will you measure success?), missing literature review (not knowing related work).
Know your limitations. Acknowledge what is out of scope. Have contingency plans for technical risks. Be specific about deliverables and success criteria.

learning_objectives: Execute development sprints with regular progress tracking
Conduct peer code reviews and technical assessments
Address technical challenges with advisor guidance
readings: Code review best practices in security tools

lecture_notes.introduction: The development phase transforms your design into a working system. Regular reviews ensure quality and catch issues early.
key_concepts: Sprint planning and execution
Security-focused code review: common vulnerabilities in security tools
Testing strategies: unit tests, integration tests, security tests
Technical documentation: API docs, architecture decision records
Progress tracking and blocker resolution
lecture_notes.demonstration: Conducting a peer code review session focusing on security-relevant code quality issues

review.value: Ironic but common: security tools with their own vulnerabilities. Review for: hardcoded credentials, insecure defaults, injection flaws in input handling, excessive privileges.
Test against known malicious samples (for detection tools), known vulnerable configs (for assessment tools), and adversarial inputs (for all tools). Measure false positive and false negative rates.

class_schedule.topic: Milestone 2: Mid-Project Review and Pivot Planning
learning_objectives: Present mid-project progress with working prototype
Assess whether the project is on track against milestones
Identify and plan for necessary pivots or scope adjustments
readings: Pivot strategies for research and development

lecture_notes.introduction: The mid-project review is a critical checkpoint. It is better to acknowledge challenges and adjust scope now than to deliver an incomplete project at the end.
key_concepts: Progress assessment against original milestones
Demo of working prototype or proof of concept
Risk reassessment: what has changed since the proposal?
Scope adjustment: reducing scope maintains quality over quantity
Revised timeline for remaining weeks
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each team presents their working prototype, demonstrates progress, and discusses challenges

review.value: Show something working, even if incomplete. A working prototype with limited features is more convincing than slides describing planned features.
Teams that honestly report challenges receive help. Teams that hide problems deliver failed projects. Mid-review is the time for transparency.

major_assignments.title: Cybersecurity Capstone Project
major_assignments.description: Design, implement, and present a comprehensive cybersecurity project that integrates knowledge from across the curriculum. Projects may include security tool development, red/blue team exercises, security architecture design, or original security research.
objectives: Demonstrate integration of cybersecurity knowledge across multiple domains
Produce a tangible deliverable with real-world applicability
Apply professional project management and documentation practices
Present findings effectively to technical and non-technical audiences

criteria: Clear problem statement with justified significance
Thorough literature review with gap identification
Quality of code, analysis, or research methodology
User guide for any developed tools
Lessons learned and future work identified
Equitable contribution from all team members
Technically sophisticated project demonstrating mastery of cybersecurity concepts

project_proposal.description: Proposal quality, literature review, and planning

milestone_reviews.description: Progress at mid-project and pre-final reviews

final_implementation.description: Technical quality and completeness of deliverable

documentation.description: Technical documentation, user guides, and retrospective

course_policies.attendance: Attendance is mandatory for all team meetings, milestone reviews, and the final presentation. Unexcused absence from a milestone review results in a 10% penalty.
course_policies.late_submissions: Milestone deliverables cannot be submitted late. Final deliverables penalized 5% per day.
course_policies.collaboration: This is a team-based course. All team members are expected to contribute equitably. Peer evaluations will be conducted at each milestone.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All code must be original or properly attributed with open-source licenses. Using others' code without attribution is plagiarism.
course_policies.lab_safety: All project work involving security testing must be conducted within approved environments. External testing requires written authorization from the target organization and faculty advisor.
